metadata.author: douglasswm
metadata.notes: Service contract: 60% completion + defects vs. scope-change defence. Tests Supply of Goods Act / fitness for purpose.

raw_documents.content: I contracted Northline Renovations on 1 August 2025 for a kitchen renovation at $4,200. The contract specified completion within 6 weeks. After 3 months the work is approximately 60% complete. The installed countertop has visible cracks, cabinet doors are misaligned, and the backsplash tiles are uneven. I have paid $2,520 (60% of contract value) in progress payments. The contractor has not appeared on site since 15 October 2025. I seek the remaining work to be completed or a refund of $1,680 to hire another contractor.

Northline Renovations confirms the project timeline was extended due to three scope change requests from Mr. Lim: (1) upgrade from laminate to quartz countertop (agreed 20 Aug), (2) addition of under-cabinet lighting (agreed 5 Sep), (3) change of backsplash material from ceramic to mosaic (agreed 18 Sep). Each change required remeasurement and reordering. The tile supplier experienced a 3-week delay. We dispute the defect claims — the countertop was inspected at installation and the crack appeared after Mr. Lim placed heavy equipment on it.

Quotation: kitchen renovation, $4,200. Scope: countertop (laminate), 12 cabinet doors, ceramic backsplash, plumbing reconnection. Timeline: 6 weeks from start. Variation orders to be priced separately.

precedents: Hong Tong Motor Trading v Hyundai Mobis Parts Distribution
